mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trajectory in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the tightening trend continues.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during periods of rising FEDFUNDS are not statistically lower than returns during flat/falling periods (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes broader economic contraction, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the effect disappears when tested on post-2010 samples excluding recessions.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is rising concurrently with elevated CPI inflation, the joint signal indicates an aggressive real-rate tightening regime that compounds valuation pressure beyond what either series alone implies, producing sharper negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days than a single-factor model would predict.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with high inflation
failure_condition: The interaction term's marginal contribution to Rank IC is not significantly greater than the sum of individual FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL effects (delta IC < 0.02) across walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a sustained decline in housing starts reflects rate-sensitive demand destruction that leads broader economic activity, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as the housing slowdown propagates to consumer spending and employment.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns show no significant negative bias following triggered HOUST declines during tightening regimes (p > 0.05), or the relationship reverses sign outside recessionary windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in industrial production reflects expanding real economic output and corporate earnings capacity, producing a short-horizon continuation effect that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO momentum are not significantly higher than following negative momentum (t-test p > 0.05) across a 5-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A retail sales acceleration exceeding 0.5% month-over-month signals resilient consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following triggered RSAFS surges are not positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with an unemployment rate below 5% jointly signal an overheating labor market that raises the probability of accelerated Fed tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as markets price in policy risk despite near-term economic strength.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE overheating signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns better than either series individually (delta Rank IC < 0.02), or effect vanishes outside tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a deceleration in real GDP growth relative to its prior quarter signals waning economic momentum that foreshadows earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter, but this effect is muted or absent in early or mid-cycle regimes.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth decelerates for 2 consecutive quarters
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following GDPC1 deceleration in late-cycle windows are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or the same pattern in early/mid-cycle windows produces equally negative returns, negating regime-specificity.
